turnaround_reason: This is a SPAC unit (combined share + warrant), not a turnaround stock. The screen surfaced it because the unit price action mimicked a turnaround pattern (deep drawdown + close to MA crossover), but SPACs trade based on trust value and de-SPAC merger probability, not operating fundamentals. Should not be analyzed as a turnaround.
bullish_points: N/A — SPAC unit, not an operating business
bearish_points: SPACs without an announced merger trade on trust value (~$10/share); any 'rally' from below $10 is typically a redemption/warrant artifact
AltEnergy Acquisition has not announced a definitive de-SPAC business combination
The 'U' suffix means it's a unit (share + warrant fraction) — illiquid and confusing for screen purposes
Could be subject to extension votes or liquidation — both outcomes don't fit 'turnaround' framing
catalyst: Any de-SPAC merger announcement or trust extension vote
suggested_trade: Skip. The screener has a bug: SPACs/units shouldn't pass this screen. Action item — add a filter in src/screener.py to exclude tickers ending in 'U', 'W', 'R' suffixes (units, warrants, rights) AND tickers with 'Acquisition Corp' / 'Holdings' in the name when sector is 'Shell Companies' or has no revenue.
would_skip_if: Always — this should never have passed the screen
